description: This observational, multi-cohort clinical proteomics study profiled plasma samples from ALS patients and controls using the Olink HT platform. Phase 1 included sporadic ALS, familial ALS, and healthy controls; Phase 2 added ALS and non-neurological controls from the Target ALS clinical cohort and was integrated using Olink bridging normalization. Differential protein abundance and pathway enrichment analyses identified reproducible ALS-associated plasma protein signatures across cohorts, with NEFL emerging as a robust biomarker after adjustment for age and sex. Muscle, actin, and muscle fiber-related pathways were consistently upregulated, supporting biomarker discovery, cross-cohort validation, and investigation of ALS disease biology.
title: ALSKP Olink Dataset
